response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that contains proteins which can trigger an allergic reaction in some people  
the immune system of someone allergic to peanuts may react to the proteins in peanuts when they are in water  
the proteins in peanuts are not soluble in water and do not dissolve easily in liquid  
this makes it difficult for someone allergic to peanuts to live underwater where the proteins would be present in the water  

Sec. 2  
the body of a person allergic to peanuts is not adapted to handle the proteins in water  
the proteins in peanuts are not absorbed by the body in the same way as they are in food  
the body cannot process the proteins in water and they do not cause an allergic reaction in the same way as they do in food
